_meta.what_firm_does: Suno builds the world's first creative entertainment platform where anyone can make music, and is the leader in AI music. Backed by Menlo, Lightspeed, Matrix, NVIDIA's venture arm. Fast pace, high ownership. Onsite in Cambridge MA or Chelsea NYC.
_meta.jd_wants: Source P0 roles across engineering/product/design/biz ops/GTM/exec; Boolean + LinkedIn Recruiter; build pipelines for hard-to-fill roles; candidate outreach/engagement + qualify; track sourcing metrics; talent mapping/competitive intel; thrive in ambiguity; passion for music + diverse teams. 3+ yrs sourcing, preferably tech/startup; Ashby + CRM.
_meta.drafted_by: Opus 4.8 in Jamie's voice, mined from voice corpus + feedback rules
_meta.honest_gap_note: Dedicated sourcing/Boolean/LinkedIn Recruiter at scale is a stretch vs Jamie's Kronos sourcing + outreach. Answers lean on real Kronos pipeline win + transferable analytical/outreach strengths, candid about depth.
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why are you interested in this role?: I love the detective side of sourcing—understanding a role well enough to picture exactly who'd thrive in it, then finding and reaching that person in a way that earns a genuine reply. At Kronos Research I sourced for a hard-to-fill international role and built a pipeline through personalized outreach that increased applications by 566% in two months, and that taught me sourcing isn't about volume—it's about resonance. Doing that across engineering, product, design, and GTM at an early-stage company like Suno, where the people I help find literally shape what gets built, is exactly the kind of high-impact work I want.
Tell us about your sourcing experience: I'll be candid—my deepest sourcing experience is at Kronos, where I owned an international role end-to-end: writing the JD, researching competitive comp, and running compelling outreach that drove a 566% jump in applications. I also organized recruiting events and moderated C-suite panels reaching 230+ students. I haven't run LinkedIn Recruiter Boolean searches at scale across P0 technical roles yet, so I'd treat that as a fast ramp—but the core muscles (understanding a role, building a pipeline, writing outreach people actually answer, and tracking what converts) are ones I've already used and genuinely enjoy.
How do you use data in your work?: My greatest strength is relying on data to solve problems and build sustainable strategy around what stakeholders need. At InGenius I track enrollment and engagement metrics to optimize strategy and conversion, and at NextGen I analyzed 2,000+ employee responses to find what really drives engagement. In sourcing, I'd apply that same instinct: map the talent market, track outreach response and conversion rates, and iterate on messaging and channels based on what the data actually shows—not just gut feel.
Why Suno?: Music is one of the most human things there is, and I love that Suno is trying to give everyone—from grandmothers to Grammy winners—the joy of making it. I'm drawn to early-stage, fast-moving teams where ambiguity is the norm; my OD consulting work is ambiguous by design, and I thrive in it. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology means I treat sourcing as a system to study and improve, and I'd be excited to help build the team behind a genuinely new creative medium.
